title: quercus geminata
intro: Quercus geminata, also known as the Siam cherry or Thai cherry, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family, native to Thailand and parts of Southeast Asia. The bark and leaves of the Quercus geminata plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus geminata include reducing fever, relieving headaches and body aches, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, tincture, and powder, which can be used to make infusions, capsules, or topical applications.
